patch.value: Persistent derived read index: serve list/context/next/get-by-filter from an O(result) index instead of scanning all item files

Core scale-out child: today every read enumerates .agents/pm/<type>/*.toon. Lands as SDK primitives (pm-usfg alignment) so CLI/MCP inherit. Duplicate check: semantic-index/ledger items (vector refresh ledger) cover embeddings only, not metadata reads.

Read commands consult a rebuildable, gitignored derived index (format per ADR pm-bl8x) maintained transactionally with item writes; index is never authoritative — pm can always rebuild it from .toon files and detects staleness automatically; benchmark shows list/context/next p95 < 1s at 1M items; falls back to full scan transparently when index is absent; zero behavior change to outputs

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Wave-2 entry scoping 2026-07-10 (planning pass). This item gates wave 2 (pm-3hps feature store depends on it), carries est 900m / risk high, and had no decomposition on record; staging it de-risks entry. Proposed slices, each independently landable with zero output change:
(S1) Rebuild + staleness substrate — gitignored derived-index directory, manifest keyed on (item file mtime+size+hash watermark, schema version, index format version per ADR pm-bl8x); `pm reindex` builds it; every read path detects staleness and transparently falls back to full scan. No read path consumes the index yet; ships the benchmark harness (synthetic 10k/100k/1M item corpora) so later slices have a regression baseline. This substrate is the shared foundation pm-3hps's signal store also needs (its design note already commits to derived/rebuildable) — landing S1 first lets wave-2 items proceed in parallel afterwards.
(S2) list/get-by-filter served from the index — the pure metadata projections (status/type/tags/priority/updated_at windows) with p95 target at 1M items; scan fallback proves equivalence in tests (index-on vs index-off golden outputs).
(S3) context/next served from the index — join with claim state and dep edges; blocked-companion counts come from the same edge table (fixes the class of GH-489 drift at the source).
(S4) Transactional maintenance on the write path — mutations update the index in the same commit boundary as the .toon write, with crash-safety = staleness detection from S1 (worst case: rebuild). Only after S4 does the index become continuously warm; S1-S3 tolerate lazy rebuild.
Decision needed at wave-2 entry (maintainer or synthesis pass): accept this slicing and start S1, or descope wave 2 to pm-3hps-on-scan (feature store computes signals via full scan first, index adoption later). Recommendation: S1 first — it is ~150-200m of the 900, unblocks parallelism, and avoids building the feature store twice.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Tranche evidence: delivered versioned per-directory signatures and a rebuildable cache fast path that bypasses per-item stat work while preserving source-scan fallback, staleness invalidation, index-on/off equivalence, race handling, and on-read-hook semantics. Focused cache tests passed 13/13 and the 10k local scale gate passed. Manual 100k SDK list/get are 923/440ms. This does not yet satisfy the full feature's 1M sub-second target or transactional write-path maintenance, so the canonical feature remains open for S3/S4 rather than being prematurely closed.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Address PR #525 stale derived-index correctness finding

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR #525 P1 accepted: directory mtime/ctime/size cannot detect an in-place item edit, so directory signatures alone are insufficient staleness proof. Correctness must be restored without pretending the full transactional index feature is complete. Duplicate check reused this canonical index feature.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR #525 review disposition: optional cache access is now null-safe. Greptile's stale-read harness used an in-place direct file write; accepted ADR pm-bl8x explicitly excludes direct edits from supported mutations, requires pm reindex after recovery edits, and guarantees supported pm mutations use atomic replacement that changes the directory signature. Existing tests cover the no-stat fast path, directory invalidation, and forceSourceScan recovery. Full 100% coverage and 10k scale gate pass; the canonical 1M/transactional feature remains open.

author: maintainer-agent
message: PR review disposition complete; keep canonical 1M/transactional index feature open

author: maintainer-agent
message: Encode accepted ADR behavior in test

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Second-pass cache test request: encode the accepted ADR behavior explicitly by asserting an unsupported in-place edit remains cached until forceSourceScan/reindex, then returns updated source content. No runtime behavior change.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Second review-loop evidence: focused cache test now explicitly proves the accepted ADR behavior for an unsupported in-place edit (derived snapshot remains stable) and the required forceSourceScan/reindex recovery (updated source returned). Runtime index semantics unchanged; full coverage passes.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Second review-loop cache contract evidence complete; keep full 1M/transactional feature open

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Design constraint from pm-4k6b algorithm (18) (2026-07-12): the persistent read index must support DESCENDING-IMPACT ITERATION with a cheap upper bound for the unseen remainder per source (activation-sorted slates, distance-ordered ANN streams), not just point/filter lookup — threshold-algorithm early termination (anytime context assembly, wave-3 packer pm-55ra) is locked out if the layout only serves exact-match reads. Costs nothing extra if slates are stored sorted by folded score, which the pm-4k6b write-time fold (6) already implies.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Design constraints from pm-4k6b directed pass 2026-07-13 (recorded BEFORE implementation, alongside the existing impact-ordered-layout constraint from algorithm (18)): (a) the edge-kind vocabulary the index stores/traverses must be EXTENSIBLE — algorithm (22) lineage-aware relevance continuity adds copied_from/split_from/split_into/merged_into/superseded_by as first-class typed edges with kind-specific damping; a hardcoded closed edge-kind enum in the index layout would lock this out; (b) row schema should not preclude an OPTIONAL era tag — algorithm (20) era segmentation partitions the timeline into a small era table and consumers discount/expand by era; the index does not need to compute eras, only not lock out an extra small column later. Both are layout non-blockers, zero behavior change for wave 2.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Latency evidence 2026-07-15 (review pass #89, warm repeated runs, this repo ~1.8k+ items across type folders): pm --version 82ms (startup healthy, irp1/gt82 relief holding) vs pm list --limit 5 --json 666ms · pm next --json 882ms · pm context --json 952ms · pm search runtime --limit 3 919ms. The maintainer directive explicitly requires everything to feel instant (vector search exempted); at 2k items the full-store scan already costs ~600-900ms per query command, so the O(result) derived read index this item owns is the single highest-leverage perf item on the active backlog. Measurement method: date +%s%N wall-clock around a warm second run.

value.author: codex-audit-20260715
value.text: Review pass 91 performance evidence (2026-07-15, current local build, three manual wall-clock runs): version 56.8-71.5ms; get 484.7-1091ms; list-open 727-993ms; context 949-987ms; search 803-898ms; next 913-1663ms. TOON context --limit 10 was 4255 bytes versus 6141 JSON; list-open --limit 20 was 3950 versus 5213 JSON. This independently reinforces the existing persistent derived-index and explicit warm/cold latency-budget scope; no parallel item created.

value.text: Derived-index tranche evidence 2026-07-21: the SDK now has a shared cursor-stamped, versioned, rebuildable signal snapshot substrate with derived_index/scan_fallback provenance, strict stale/corrupt detection, atomic replacement, and authoritative item joins. This advances the shared S1/S3 contract but does not claim the full 1M transactional index acceptance; pm-801d remains open for continuously maintained write-path indexing and the 1M p95 target.

value.author: codex-derived-context-20260721
value.text: Scale-gate diagnostic 2026-07-21: current branch 10k check fails the pre-existing get token budgets (CLI 1042>322; SDK 701>288) plus load-sensitive latency. A clean detached main d38fb47e reproduces the same deterministic token failures (CLI 1042, SDK 701) and also exceeded CLI create latency (best 322>310); main context measured min 1168/p95 1195 CLI and min 698/p95 1128 SDK. The first branch run overlapped packed smoke, explaining broad contention; a sequential branch run still showed system-wide CLI slowdown, not a context-only regression. Product token ceiling remains satisfied, but committed regression budgets need canonical follow-up under this item rather than blind baseline updates.

This rolling feature remains open for transactional write-path maintenance, full list/get/context index consumption, and the 1M p95 acceptance target; the reproduced scale/token baseline remains documented here.

patch.value: Incremental index mutation and recovery coverage

value.note: cursor staleness fallback and atomic persistence coverage

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Implementation evidence 2026-07-21: promoted a public SDK-owned derived-index mutation transaction with a small base manifest, cross-process writer lock, shared source cursor, and one atomic collapsed delta spanning metadata/body/collection projections. create/update/move/delete/copy/restore now keep supported mutations warm without whole-index rewrites; corrupt/torn/mismatched delta or manifest state invalidates only rebuildable files and forces authoritative source-scan recovery. Exact all-source coverage passes 100/100/100/100 (6071 tests, 39852 lines); strict static/docstring/SDK-boundary/token gates pass; package-first installed dogfood passed 88 commands. 10k measurement improved the initial whole-envelope design from CLI create 782ms/197.7MB and claim 684ms/197.0MB to create 357ms with normal memory and claim within budget; SDK create/claim both returned within budget. Remaining gate failures are the already-reproduced main-line get token budgets (CLI 1042>322, SDK 701>288) plus the tight CLI-create floor (main previously 322>310; this run 357>310), so the rolling 1M acceptance remains open rather than being falsely closed.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: A transactional derived index cannot rewrite O(workspace) JSON envelopes on each mutation: a cursor-bound base manifest plus collapsed atomic delta preserves warm-read correctness while making mutation cost O(changed paths). Corrupt derived state must invalidate eagerly; directory metadata alone is not a safe substitute for a rejected delta.

author: codex-root
message: Transactional 10k derived-index tranche complete in PR #636; the documented main-line get token budgets and 1M p95 acceptance remain open.

value.author: codex-root-20260722

Scope is CLI/SDK context and next integration, continuously maintained derived signals, deterministic explainability, bounded token packing, and scan-fallback parity. All-status duplicate review reused this canonical three-item lineage; no parallel context item was created.

value.author: codex-root-20260722
value.text: Verification evidence 2026-07-22: the effective derived cursor advances across transactional deltas, invalid delta state self-invalidates, and context/next consume cursor-bound rows with scan parity under exact coverage. Fresh 10k context p95 is 583ms CLI / 207ms SDK. The canonical feature remains open because its acceptance explicitly requires 1M p95 proof and the full scale gate still reports the pre-existing get token baseline failures (CLI 1,042>322; SDK 701>288).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implementation evidence 2026-07-23: added a rebuildable SQLite metadata query index with atomic cursor-bound rebuilds, transactional create/update/delete/move maintenance, corruption and external-mutation fallback, and a bounded default-list fast path. Standard pm get containers now report child counts/statuses/continuations without embedding child samples, restoring the strict 10k token budgets. pnpm benchmark:scale:check passes for CLI and SDK at 10k; exact coverage is 100/100/100/100. The feature remains open because its acceptance still requires list/context/next p95 below 1s at 1M items and full read-surface adoption.

value.author: claude-code-agent

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Correction to 2026-07-23 implementation evidence: automatic staleness fallback covers supported pm mutations and source replacements that advance the directory signature. Unsupported direct in-place edits can remain cached by design and require forceSourceScan or pm reindex for authoritative recovery, consistent with ADR pm-bl8x and the existing regression test.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review feedback evidence 2026-07-23: CodeRabbit correctly requested clarification of unsupported direct in-place edit recovery. Append-only correction ac2fad26 now states the forceSourceScan/reindex requirement; the inline thread was acknowledged and resolved.
